title: Chapter 286 – Lotus (5)
content:
Slash—

The sword of Sky-Jade Sect Leader Geom Woon-bi carved through the night air.

Its force grazed exposed skin in passing, tremendous and alive. As the blade descended in a sweeping arc, the moonlight itself seemed to ripple.

It was swordsmanship beyond imagination. The footwork harmonizing with each stroke was entirely original.

His muscles and the white trajectory danced as one. An unbroken line, continuous and fluid.

'What is that...!'

A restrained sharpness lived within the sword wind, along with the bearing of a noble house. The depth of his power was impossible to fathom.

They say a master's skill reflects the life they've lived. Inspiration is bound to existence itself.

A refined swordsman's single stroke contains all their habits and temperament. A martial artist from a prestigious family, trained in the art of reading energy, could perceive this even more clearly.

Young Family Head Gongya Jeong felt exactly that. She sensed a profound loneliness.

She wanted to see more closely.

'No — this violates martial etiquette...!'

Just as Gongya Jeong was about to squeeze her eyes shut.

Rustle.

The sword that had been carving freely through the moonlight faltered. The sleeve slid uselessly down past the blue veins of his forearm.

Perhaps one of the servants accompanying her father had gasped aloud. The sword dance sweeping through the moonlight came to an end.

"I've been rude. This isn't my home."

The sect leader said.

Shing.

Gongya Jeong stared blankly at Geom Woon-bi's elegant sheathing of his blade. In this moment, something deeper than the moonlight lingered in the air.

Despite her resolve not to look further, she had to suppress a profound sense of regret.

An emotion she hadn't felt in a long time rose within her. It wasn't romantic attraction between a man and woman.

The heir of a noble house had been shaken to her core by a wandering swordsman's blade work.

At a time when she carried such a heavy burden — to an extent she herself could not fully reckon with.

—I extended the invitation with urgency, considering the eldest lady's concerns. What fortune that he accepted, despite our modest connection. I assure you, he will prove to be a rare opportunity.

Teacher Gao's words flashed through the young family head's mind.

"...To say 'the light beneath the lamp is dim' doesn't begin to capture it. We failed to see what was right under our noses... Has our judgment grown rigid from years of complacency? In our youth, we used to verify everything with our own eyes."

The Gongya family head, standing opposite Geom Woon-bi, spoke. His voice carried a deep resonance.

Moonlight fell upon the bright red robe draped over his large frame. His stern eyes held a dark gleam.

"I didn't know a swordsman of your caliber existed among our young family head's peers. I apologize for inadvertently witnessing your practice. I was captivated."

It was a straightforward apology. His conduct carried unmistakable class.

Jeong Yeon-shin shook his head as he reined in the traces of energy that had leaked out.

The middle-aged man before him could detect black-rank internal energy from the Luminous Wheel Technique. He had to suppress it considerably.

Though the Gongya family was called a noble house only within Shanxi, as the head of a great martial clan, the man's martial prowess should not be easily overwhelmed by a Desolate Fortress black-rank.

"The fault belongs to a guest who chose his spot poorly. I have a habit of practicing wherever I find space."

He said, smoothing the sword belt at his waist with his fingertips.

The corners of the Gongya family head's thick lips curved upward.

"It is our house's failure not to have recognized a refined swordsman sooner. I ought to treat you with proper generosity, but may I ask the name of that sword technique?"

"I was merely swinging as instinct dictated. It doesn't have a proper form or name yet."

Jeong Yeon-shin answered vaguely.

It was still far from complete. He hadn't properly refined the rough sword path.

He had only extracted the refined qualities from the Orthodox Divine Sword — something an enlightened monkey would use to chop firewood.

His aim was to create a fifth sword technique.

An insight to make the Thunder-Annihilation Sword Style even more complete. For now, he possessed only the initial spark.

Ultimately, this was a mission to confront an extraordinarily rare peerless master. He couldn't rely on techniques designed for short, decisive battles.

Now that he could no longer easily generate protective energy, he needed a stable counter-method.

'A sword form to compensate for the weaknesses of burst techniques.'

He aimed to imbue the newly born Thunder-Annihilation Sword Style with the bearing of a noble house.

He planned to artificially graft the refinement that was said to come only with time.

If completed before facing Zhuge Clan Leader, it would be ideal — but that was impossible to predict.

A sword technique to fully embody the mystical skill of the Ability Law Luminous Wheel Technique could not be perfected easily. Supreme inspiration had to be contained in every trajectory.

'They say you learn while teaching.'

The Gongya Family was a prestigious house. He thought there might be more to gain here.

Just as he had unexpectedly distilled the Jeong Family's Orthodox Divine Sword into a single form during his stay.

Then.

"No form or name...?"

The Gongya family head repeated Geom Woon-bi's words with a peculiar expression.

After regarding the young swordsman for a long moment, he smoothed his sleeve and spoke again.

He suppressed the competitive spirit that had risen within the swordsman in him. The gap in age and station was too great.

"The night has grown late. Forgive me for not seeking you out sooner after welcoming such an esteemed guest."

"The food was a heavenly delicacy."

Jeong Yeon-shin spoke the truth. The corner of the dignified Gongya family head's mouth rose faintly.

"Feel free to stay at our house for as long as you wish. You're welcome to visit without notice. Make yourself entirely at home."

Jeong Yeon-shin responded with a cupped fist salute.

Gongya Jeong, standing before the annex porch, was still silently watching him.

The Gongya young family head. The noble house's eldest daughter. Pale moonlight settled upon the face from which the flush of wine had faded.

***

https://discord.gg/XC9DTsTQ9Z

***

Jeong Yeon-shin received a visit from Gongya Jeong as soon as day broke.

Scholar Gao Jin, who had quietly emerged behind her, waved his fist with a bright face. He was like a well-mannered version of Hyeon Won-chang.

"I humbly request instruction from Sect Leader Geom."

The eldest daughter bowed formally. A strand of hair fell beside her nape.

Her hands formed a roof-like clasp rather than a martial salute. It was the folk tradition of the cupped-hand bow — a deliberate gesture of humility.

The intent was to show respect.

Jeong Yeon-shin had received such a greeting before. From the Tang siblings of the Sichuan Tang Clan.

When they had confirmed the key points of Myriad Heavens Flower Rain, they offered this same salute, calling him their benefactor. They were among the most talented individuals he had ever encountered.

They could be called human. He had even felt a strange sense of kinship with them.

'I hope they're doing well.'

He hoped the Gongya young family head's qualities might at least approach the shadow of theirs.

Jeong Yeon-shin slowly parted his lips.

"The family head's martial arts seemed formidable. I'm curious why you came to me specifically."

"To broaden my horizons. Sect Leader Geom's sword dance was astonishing beyond words. If any of my previous remarks or conduct displeased you, I ask your forgiveness."

Gongya Jeong's manner of speech had shifted to formal politeness.

There was no need to make a fuss over it. For martial artists in times of famine, martial prowess was equivalent to status.

Even the most rigidly maintained hierarchies lose their meaning in the face of hunger.

He quietly nodded.

"Since I enjoyed a fine meal, a guest ought to earn his keep."

Contrary to his words, he had skipped breakfast.

This was an important moment. He walked alongside Gongya Jeong to an open space.

Since it was already a quiet place, they didn't bother going to the training grounds. Jeong Yeon-shin told her to draw her sword, suppressing his impatience.

"Yes."

Gongya Jeong unsheathed her blade.

At the same time, Jeong Yeon-shin's gaze traveled from her arms down to her ankles. He was reading the contours of her muscles and the shape of her energy waves.

Then he moved his lips slightly — a sound transmission directed at Gongya Jeong.

The corner of her mouth, which had been listening in silence, trembled in the sunlight.

"How can this possibly be a secret technique..."

"Secret...?"

Jeong Yeon-shin tilted his head slightly and urged her on. What he had just explained was no secret technique.

It was a "way of using the body" derived from brief insight. He had reverse-engineered a sword technique and footwork suited to the Gongya young family head's physique after a single observation.

'With long limbs, she can expand the sword's range considerably. The left-hand muscles are weaker compared to the right, but... she should be accustomed to that imbalance by now. She needs to fully utilize the deftly packed muscle bundles and the meridians in her wrists.'

The Jeong Family's Dynamic Technique grandmaster had always emphasized utilizing one's innate physique.

For Jeong Yeon-shin, born into a third-rate martial family, it had been the only path to improving his martial arts.

Naturally, he regarded people who squandered superior movements as monkeys.

"Try it."

He gestured with his chin, arms folded. It was a posture that had become second nature by now.

Gongya Jeong took no offense.

She stared at the tip of her sword raised in a middle stance, then with a short shout, thrust the blade horizontally.

Her red sleeve fluttered wide.

Slash!

Jeong Yeon-shin was silent for a moment.

The sword's energy waves swept the dust away. A calm stillness followed.

As the ochre dust fully dispersed, he spoke again.

"There seems to be a misunderstanding. Let me explain once more."

Jeong Yeon-shin said casually.

This was a martial clan. Rather than criticizing the eldest daughter's talent, it was better to reconsider his own teaching.

Even a roadside pebble rolls when kicked. There was no reason to assume failure from the start.

He sent another sound transmission to the Gongya young family head, and she silently extended her sword once more.

Slash—

The moment a straight white line was etched in the air, the atmosphere shuddered faintly. A stroke that would look formidable to any observer.

The movement of muscles and energy from her toes to the sword's edge created an opaque distortion in the air.

Jeong Yeon-shin's eyelashes trembled.

"If you relax your right shoulder blade a bit more while channeling the energy through your wrist's inner gate point as I described... the power of your horizontal slash will increase. Far more than this."

"I've actually been pondering this for some time, and Sect Leader Geom's words give me certainty. I've already entered the first stage of achievement, so I can train for ten years to reach mastery. I can only express my gratitude for such surprising and invaluable guidance."

Shing.

Gongya Jeong sheathed her sword in an instant and raised both hands. Her conduct revealed an unyielding spirit.

Her casual mention of ten years didn't ring hollow.

She was like bright marble — someone who would polish herself over long years to achieve greatness. A late bloomer that Jeong Yeon-shin could never comprehend.

"..."

The current Radiant Demon Wing Leader fell into quiet despair. The shock was immense.

He had to force his lips apart.

"You'll... do well."

The Gongya young family head inhabited a completely different timeframe.

The edge of a distant cliff came to mind. She was a stone slowly worn smooth by wind over the passage of years.

Jeong Yeon-shin thought that an ephemeral being like himself could never fathom the patience of a pebble.

***

His eyes felt hollow. That was the only word for it.

Jeong Yeon-shin greeted the morning sunlight with bleary eyes. The light seeping through the paper windows seemed to lick his skin, as though mocking him.

He received Gongya Jeong, who had come to pay her morning respects, with an impassive expression.

"Sect Leader Geom, if there is any discomfort in your sleeping arrangements, please let me know at once. Our house has many skilled servants. Changing the bedding is a trivial matter for them."

"It's because I tasted defeat for the first time in my life..."

"So you engaged in mental cultivation even while lying in bed. This foolish Gongya should learn from your discipline. Spending the first month of the year with an exemplary swordsman like yourself, I feel this year's fortune will be auspicious."

Her black hair, neatly tied back since morning, caught his eye.

The clean luster flowing through it — washed and dried at some impossibly early hour — struck him as the very embodiment of effort.

She seemed to practice energy circulation at the crack of dawn. The standard method for accumulating power. An admirable discipline.

'Mental cultivation...'

Jeong Yeon-shin privately swallowed his doubts. Surely she wasn't mocking him.

"What brings you here?"

"I mentioned the preliminary battle for the martial arts tournament before. People from prestigious families are gathering at our house. That's why the banquet is being held here. As I said earlier, making acquaintances among them will be of great benefit to you, Sect Leader Geom. Many powerful figures will be present."

"The preliminary for the martial arts tournament..."

After thinking for a moment, Jeong Yeon-shin nodded.

"I'll attend the banquet."

"A wise decision. However..."

Gongya Jeong's expression, which had briefly brightened, grew shadowed.

"The hostility toward our family runs deep. There is even a brute from a sect founded by former bandits. They are all recognized as prestigious families in this vast Shanxi province now, so some may act rudely toward you, Sect Leader Geom."

"Will all the tournament participants be there?"

"Yes."

"I've heard there are many sects straddling the line between orthodox and unorthodox, leaning toward the demonic side..."

"They are the majority. Shanxi has long been a land where military power held sway. The intense pressure on martial clans made it difficult to sustain a sect on the orthodox path. Our family's history was riddled with hardships as well... Many of the bandits led by generals who fled after being framed by the military, or martial artists who lived by the sword, are wary of the Great Ming Code. You could say they're half demonic path."

"Then there's nothing to worry about."

"Of course, Sect Leader Geom's swordsmanship is far more refined than theirs, but still, demonic sects..."

"Young Family Head."

"Yes?"

"You'll one day stand unrivaled. I see it in your effort."

"That's far too much praise..."

Eldest daughter Gongya Jeong offered a faint smile.

Jeong Yeon-shin did not smile.

If there was no one left to fight, that person would truly be invincible.

The mental fatigue accumulated overnight weighed heavily on him. He even worried whether his maternal grandfather lived with this same feeling at all times.

'I should treat him more warmly.'

He recalled the pure blood cloth tucked in a corner of his luggage. The symbol of Blood Flame Cult nobility.

He had to surpass his black-rank seniors with overwhelming, undefeated achievements. Failure was not an option for the current Radiant Demon Wing Leader.
